channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Getting AI right in 2025: control, control, control
item.description: Businesses face challenges with AI adoption; focusing on control, data privacy, and specialized tools ensures success.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon is spending billions on expanding its data centers in Georgia
item.description: Amazon Web Services confirms plans to invest $11 billion in Georgia; the state has now received $18.5 billion since 2010.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tech industry sounds alarm over US AI Export Control Framework
item.description: The Biden administration’s recently introduced “Export Control Framework for Artificial Intelligence Diffusion” has sparked a heated debate in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: En marcha un test de estrés en español para medir los sesgos de la IA generativa
item.description: Las aplicaciones basadas en grandes modelos de lenguaje como GPT -el modelo detrás de ChatGPT-, Gemini o Llama han hecho posible que cualquier usuario ‘converse’ directamente con la inteligencia artificial (IA).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Website certificates that expire every six weeks? What IT should know
item.description: Industry forces — led by Apple and Google — are pushing for a sharp acceleration of how often website certificates must be updated, but the stated security reason is raising an awful lot of eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Think Ryzen 9000 CPUs are still a bit pricey? AMD just revealed a cheaper Zen 5 chip that could be perfect for your budget PC build
item.description: We don’t have a launch price for the new Ryzen 9600 yet, but we can hope for the CPU to retail at around $200.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DualSense Edge Controller Price Drop: A Game-Changer for PS5 Owners?
item.description: Sony has dropped the price of its premium DualSense Edge controller, making it more affordable for PS5 owners in the UK.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Silicon Valley homeless nonprofit sues Microsoft over disrupted email access
item.description: The nonprofit LifeMoves claims the disruption has made it difficult to connect homeless people with services.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Varian, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta's fact-checking changes put company under political microscope
item.description: Meta’s decision to eliminate its fact-checking program marks a sharp reversal for the major social media company, quickly prompting questions over the firm’s direction as President-elect Trump heads back to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: New Version of Gleam Released with Performance Improvements
item.description: Gleam 1.7 has arrived, featuring faster record updates and a dynamic coder for custom types, along with several other performance and package publishing improvements." charset="UTF-8
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lost your contacts on your phone? Here's how to get them back
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides two methods to restore your contacts for iPhone and Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta ending fact-checking program: Zuckerberg
item.description: Social media giant Meta announced a series of changes to its content moderation policies Tuesday, including the elimination of its fact-checking program, in what CEO Mark Zuckerberg said was an effort to embrace free speech.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia's new GPU series led an avalanche of entertainment-related announcements at CES
item.description: Nvidia unveiled its GeForce RTX 50 Series desktop and laptop GPUs — powered by its new Blackwell artificial intelligence chip — kicking off a string of entertainment-related AI announcements and discussions at the trade show, including several terse conversations about regulation of the budding tech
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sam Altman offers clues about where OpenAI is headed
item.description: On Sunday OpenAI cofounder and CEO Sam Altman published a blog post titled Reflections about his company’s progress—and the speed bumps along the way—during its first nine years.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cybertruck tracking in Las Vegas case raises privacy concerns
item.description: Elon Musk commented on the explosion shortly after it happened, saying it was not an issue with the Cybertruck itself.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government
item.description: Guy Cavallo has been the Office of Personnel Management’s CIO since 2020 and also worked at SBA, TSA and began his career working for the Senate. The post OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agencies required to share custom software under new law
item.description: The SHARE IT Act, now law, builds on previous efforts to ensure agencies are sharing common software code across government. The post Agencies required to share custom software under new law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
